story.title: Leonie V. and Joslyn (Junior) Chance Oral History
story.description: The interview chronicles Leonie V. Chance's life journey from Jamaica to the United States, emphasizing her family-centered life. Raised in a culturally diverse environment, Leonie moved to Hartford and eventually Bloomfield, Connecticut, for better educational opportunities for her children. Her story highlights the role of education, hard work, and familial support in achieving the "American dream." She reflects on cultural integration, familial responsibilities, and community life, particularly noting Bloomfield's welcoming atmosphere and evolution over time. Leonie underscores the importance of her faith and the richness of her multi-racial family connections.
